# Skill: customize-opencode

<!--
  Built-in skill. Name and description are registered in code at
  packages/opencode/src/skill/index.ts (see CUSTOMIZE_OPENCODE_SKILL_NAME
  and CUSTOMIZE_OPENCODE_SKILL_DESCRIPTION). The body below becomes the
  skill's content.
-->

# Customizing opencode

opencode validates its own config strictly and refuses to start when a field
is wrong. The shapes below cover the common surface area, but they are a
**summary, not the source of truth**.

## Full schema reference

The authoritative list of every config option — with field types, enums,
defaults, and descriptions — lives in the published JSON Schema:

**<https://opencode.ai/config.json>**

If a field is not documented in this skill, or you need to confirm an exact
shape before writing config, **fetch that URL and read the schema directly**
rather than guessing. opencode hard-fails on invalid config, so the cost of a
wrong shape is a broken startup.

Independently, every `opencode.json` should declare
`"$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json"` so the user's editor catches
mistakes as they type.

## Applying changes

Config is loaded once when opencode starts and is not hot-reloaded. After
saving changes to `opencode.json`, an agent file, a skill, a plugin, or any
other config-time file, **tell the user to quit and restart opencode** for
the changes to take effect. The running session will keep using the
already-loaded config until then.

## Where files live

| Scope                         | Path                                                                                                                      |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Project config                | `./opencode.json`, `./opencode.jsonc`, or `.opencode/opencode.json` (opencode walks up from the cwd to the worktree root) |
| Global config                 | `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json` (NOT `~/.opencode/`)                                                                   |
| Project agents                | `.opencode/agent/<name>.md` or `.opencode/agents/<name>.md`                                                               |
| Global agents                 | `~/.config/opencode/agent(s)/<name>.md`                                                                                   |
| Project skills                | `.opencode/skill(s)/<name>/SKILL.md`                                                                                      |
| Global skills                 | `~/.config/opencode/skill(s)/<name>/SKILL.md`                                                                             |
| External skills (auto-loaded) | `~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, `~/.agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`                                                    |

Configs from each scope are deep-merged. Project overrides global. Unknown
top-level keys in `opencode.json` are rejected with `ConfigInvalidError`.

## opencode.json

Every field is optional.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "username": "string",
  "model": "provider/model-id",
  "small_model": "provider/model-id",
  "default_agent": "agent-name",
  "shell": "/bin/zsh",
  "logLevel": "DEBUG" | "INFO" | "WARN" | "ERROR",
  "share": "manual" | "auto" | "disabled",
  "autoupdate": true | false | "notify",
  "snapshot": true,
  "instructions": ["AGENTS.md", "docs/style.md"],

  "skills": {
    "paths": [".opencode/skills", "/abs/path/to/skills"],
    "urls": ["https://example.com/.well-known/skills/"]
  },

  "agent": {
    "my-agent": {
      "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6",
      "mode": "subagent",
      "description": "...",
      "permission": { "edit": "deny" }
    }
  },

  "command": {
    "deploy": { "description": "...", "prompt": "..." }
  },

  "provider": {
    "anthropic": { "options": { "apiKey": "..." } }
  },
  "disabled_providers": ["openai"],
  "enabled_providers": ["anthropic"],

  "mcp": {
    "playwright": {
      "type": "local",
      "command": ["npx", "-y", "@playwright/mcp"],
      "enabled": true,
      "env": {}
    },
    "remote-thing": {
      "type": "remote",
      "url": "https://...",
      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer ..." }
    }
  },

  "plugin": [
    "opencode-gemini-auth",
    "opencode-foo@1.2.3",
    "./local-plugin.ts",
    ["opencode-bar", { "option": "value" }]
  ],

  "permission": {
    "edit": "deny",
    "bash": { "git *": "allow", "*": "ask" }
  },

  "formatter": false,
  "lsp": false,

  "experimental": {
    "primary_tools": ["edit"],
    "mcp_timeout": 30000
  },

  "tool_output": { "max_lines": 200, "max_bytes": 8192 },

  "compaction": { "auto": true, "tail_turns": 15 }
}
```

Shape notes worth being explicit about:

- `model` always carries a provider prefix: `"anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"`.
- `skills` is an object with `paths` and/or `urls`, not an array.
- `agent` is an object keyed by agent name, not an array.
- `plugin` is an array of strings or `[name, options]` tuples, not an object.
- `mcp[name].command` is an array of strings, never a single string. `type` is required.
- `permission` is either a string action or an object keyed by tool name.

## Skills

opencode's skill loader scans for `**/SKILL.md` inside skill directories. The
file is named `SKILL.md` exactly, and lives in its own folder named after the
skill:

```
.opencode/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md
```

Frontmatter:

```markdown
---
name: my-skill
description: One sentence covering what this skill does AND when to trigger it. Front-load the literal keywords or filenames the user is likely to say.
---

# My Skill

(skill body in markdown: instructions, examples, references)
```

- `name` is required, lowercase hyphen-separated, up to 64 chars, and matches the folder name.
- `description` is effectively required: skills without one are filtered out and never surfaced to the model. Cover both _what_ the skill does and _when_ to use it. Write in third person ("Use when...", not "I help with..."). Front-load concrete trigger keywords and filenames; gate with "Use ONLY when..." if the skill should stay quiet on adjacent topics.
- Optional: `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata` (string-string map).

Register skills from non-default locations via `skills.paths` (scanned
recursively for `**/SKILL.md`) and `skills.urls` (each URL serves a list of
skills).

## Agents

Two ways to define an agent. Use the file form for anything non-trivial.

### Inline (in `opencode.json`)

```json
{
  "agent": {
    "my-reviewer": {
      "description": "Reviews PRs for style violations.",
      "mode": "subagent",
      "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6",
      "permission": { "edit": "deny", "bash": "ask" },
      "prompt": "You are a strict PR reviewer..."
    }
  }
}
```

### File

```
.opencode/agent/my-reviewer.md      OR     .opencode/agents/my-reviewer.md
```

```markdown
---
description: Reviews PRs for style violations.
mode: subagent
model: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6
permission:
  edit: deny
  bash: ask
---

You are a strict PR reviewer. Focus on...
```

The file body becomes the agent's `prompt`. Do not also put `prompt:` in the
frontmatter.

`mode` is one of `"primary"`, `"subagent"`, `"all"`.

Allowed top-level frontmatter fields: `name, model, variant, description, mode,
hidden, color, steps, options, permission, disable, temperature, top_p`. Any
unknown field is silently routed into `options`.

To disable a built-in agent: `agent: { build: { disable: true } }`, or in a
file, `disable: true` in frontmatter.

`default_agent` must point to a non-hidden, primary-mode agent.

### Built-in agents

opencode ships with `build`, `plan`, `general`, `explore`, plus optionally
`scout` (gated on `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_SCOUT`). Hidden internal agents:
`compaction`, `title`, `summary`. To override a built-in's fields, define the
same key in `agent: { <name>: { ... } }`.

## Plugins

`plugin:` is an array. Each entry is one of:

```json
"plugin": [
  "opencode-gemini-auth",            // npm spec, latest
  "opencode-foo@1.2.3",              // npm spec, pinned
  "./local-plugin.ts",               // file path, relative to the declaring config
  "file:///abs/path/plugin.js",      // file URL
  ["opencode-bar", { "key": "val" }] // tuple form with options
]
```

Auto-discovered plugins (no config entry needed): any `*.ts` or `*.js` file in
`.opencode/plugin/` or `.opencode/plugins/`.

A plugin module exports `default` (or any named export) of type
`Plugin = (input: PluginInput, options?) => Promise<Hooks>`. The export is a
function, not a plain object literal, and the function returns an object
(return `{}` if there is nothing to register).

```ts
import type { Plugin } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"

export default (async ({ client, project, directory, $ }) => {
  return {
    config: (cfg) => {
      // cfg is the live merged config; mutate fields here.
    },
    "tool.execute.before": async (input, output) => {
      // mutate output.args before the tool runs
    },
  }
}) satisfies Plugin
```

Hook surface (mutate `output` in place; return `void`):

- `event(input)`: every bus event
- `config(cfg)`: once on init with the merged config
- `chat.message`, `chat.params`, `chat.headers`
- `tool.execute.before`, `tool.execute.after`
- `tool.definition`
- `command.execute.before`
- `shell.env`
- `permission.ask`
- `experimental.chat.messages.transform`, `experimental.chat.system.transform`,
  `experimental.session.compacting`, `experimental.compaction.autocontinue`,
  `experimental.text.complete`

Special object-shaped (not callbacks): `tool: { my_tool: { ... } }`,
`auth: { ... }`, `provider: { ... }`.

## MCP servers

`mcp:` is an object keyed by server name. Each server is discriminated by
`type`:

```json
{
  "mcp": {
    "playwright": {
      "type": "local",
      "command": ["npx", "-y", "@playwright/mcp"],
      "enabled": true,
      "env": { "BROWSER": "chromium" }
    },
    "github": {
      "type": "remote",
      "url": "https://...",
      "enabled": true,
      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer ${GITHUB_TOKEN}" }
    },
    "old-server": { "enabled": false }
  }
}
```

`command` is an array of strings. `type` is required. Use `enabled: false` to
disable a server inherited from a parent config.

## Permissions

```json
"permission": {
  "edit": "deny",
  "bash": { "git *": "allow", "rm *": "deny", "*": "ask" },
  "external_directory": { "~/secrets/**": "deny", "*": "allow" }
}
```

Actions: `"allow"`, `"ask"`, `"deny"`.

Per-tool value forms: `"allow"` shorthand (treated as `{"*": "allow"}`), or an
object `{ pattern: action }`. Within an object, **insertion order matters**.
opencode evaluates the LAST matching rule, so put broad rules first and narrow
rules last.

`permission: "allow"` (a string at the top level) is shorthand for "allow
everything" and is rarely what the user wants.

Known permission keys: `read, edit, glob, grep, list, bash, task,
external_directory, todowrite, question, webfetch, websearch, codesearch,
repo_clone, repo_overview, lsp, doom_loop, skill`. Some of these (`todowrite,
question, webfetch, websearch, codesearch, doom_loop`) only accept a flat
action, not a per-pattern object.

`external_directory` patterns are filesystem paths (use `~/`, absolute paths,
or globs like `~/projects/**`).

Per-agent `permission:` overrides top-level `permission:`. Plan Mode lives on
the `plan` agent's permission ruleset (`edit: deny *`).

## Escape hatches

When a user's config is broken and opencode won't start, these env vars help:

- `OPENCODE_DISABLE_PROJECT_CONFIG=1`: skip the project's local `opencode.json`
  and start from globals only. Run from the project directory, opencode loads,
  the user edits the broken file, then they restart without the flag.
- `OPENCODE_CONFIG=/path/to/file.json`: load an additional explicit config.
- `OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT='{"$schema":"https://opencode.ai/config.json"}'`:
  inject inline JSON as a final local-scope merge.
- `OPENCODE_DISABLE_DEFAULT_PLUGINS=1`: skip default plugins.
- `OPENCODE_PURE=1`: skip external plugins entirely.
- `OPENCODE_DISABLE_EXTERNAL_SKILLS=1`,
  `OPENCODE_DISABLE_CLAUDE_CODE_SKILLS=1`: skip the external skill scans under
  `~/.claude/` and `~/.agents/`.

## When proposing edits

- Validate against the schema before writing. If you are unsure of a field's
  exact shape, or the field is not covered in this skill, fetch
  `https://opencode.ai/config.json` and read the schema rather than guessing.
- Preserve `$schema` and any existing fields the user did not ask to change.
- For agent, skill, and plugin definitions, prefer creating new files in the
  correct location over inlining everything in `opencode.json`.
- If the user's existing config is malformed, point them at the env-var escape
  hatches above so they can edit from inside opencode without breaking their
  session.
- After saving any config change, remind the user to quit and restart opencode
  — running sessions keep using the already-loaded config.

**Exploring web content tools**

**Updating keybinds in TUI**

I'm trying to decide whether to update the `tui.json` keybind for `input_force_submit` to either `input_submit` or `prompt_submit`. First, I need to understand the current semantics. The existing configuration sets `vim_enter_submit=false`, which means Enter can insert a newline. The `input_force_submit` was used for Alt+Return. The current schema has `input_submit`, `prompt_submit`, and `input_newline`, so I need to review the TUI schema for descriptions and defaults, maybe using `jq` to inspect them.

2. Keep `vim.o.autoread = true` so edits from OpenCode reload correctly.
3. Reuse the recommended keymaps (`<C-a>` ask, `<C-x>` select, `go` operator) or map them under `<leader>o` to avoid conflicts with tmux bindings.
4. Run `:checkhealth opencode` after wiring it up; the check validates that OpenCode is discoverable and events are flowing.
5. Continue to use the tmux pane when you need a dedicated transcript view or when Neovim is not open—both entry points can coexist because they talk to the same OpenCode backend.

## opencode-vim and opencode.nvim together

- `ocv` replaces the terminal TUI and provides full Vim-mode ergonomics when OpenCode runs in tmux or a standalone shell.
- `scripts/bin/opencode` preserves the command name expected by `gwt-ticket`, tmux launchers, auth rotation, cross-provider hooks, and `opencode.nvim`.
- `opencode.nvim` keeps using `opencode --port`; the shim routes that to `ocv --port`, so editor-native prompts inherit the same binary and plugin stack.
- `.config/opencode/tui.json` owns TUI Vim settings such as system clipboard register sync, prompt scrolling, copy mode, and force-submit bindings.
- Copy mode is a session-view mode, not prompt insert mode: press `Esc`, then `Ctrl-x`, then `v`; select with `v` or `V`, yank with `y`, copy to the system clipboard with `Enter`, and exit with `q`.

By moving repetitive prompt + context work into Neovim we remove most of the friction highlighted in this ticket while leaving the tmux bindings available for workflows outside the editor.

## OpenCode-only Neovim workflow

`opencode.nvim` is now the primary in-editor AI layer. It owns buffer-aware prompts, visual selections, operator workflows, and the diff review loop. The tmux side pane remains useful for long transcripts and non-editor sessions; no secondary editor-assistant plugin is part of the active workflow.

There's a possibility to use the `stow --adopt` command, but that raises a concern about needing user approval before proceeding. I want to make sure that any actions taken don’t compromise the user's important information. It’s crucial to be cautious when making these kinds of changes. Getting the right permission would be key before moving forward.

**Considering diff and jq usage**

I’m thinking about using `diff -u` for comparisons, which sounds reasonable for file reading. The developer mentioned using bash with diff for git too. However, I wonder if output could reveal sensitive tokens. It might be safer to use `jq` to compare keys. To proceed, I could read both files. I’m considering `jq 'paths(scalars) as $p | [$p|join("."), getpath($p)]'` to avoid exposing any secrets. My next step is to inspect the repo source for the Claude config first.

content.summary:
User asked: “Why is our opencode config broken? The theme isn't showing as transparent, check ~/dotfiles and ensure all symlinks are present”. This task is about `~/dotfiles` OpenCode configuration and stow symlink health.

Loaded `customize-opencode` skill because this edits/fixes OpenCode config. Key skill constraints used: global config lives at `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`; OpenCode validates config strictly; after config changes the running OpenCode session must be quit/restarted because config is loaded once.

Initial OpenCode file discovery:
- Source config dir is `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/opencode/`.
- Important files found/read:
  - `.config/opencode/opencode.json`
  - `.config/opencode/tui.json`
  - `.config/opencode/themes/transparent.json`
  - plugins under `.config/opencode/plugin/`
- `opencode.json` is valid JSON, uses `$schema: https://opencode.ai/config.json`, model `openai/gpt-5.5`, small model `anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5`, plugin list includes `@tarquinen/opencode-dcp@latest`, `opencode-pty`, `opencode-vibeguard`, `./plugin/nvim-open.ts`, permissions `allow`, and style agents `precise`, `balanced`, `creative`, `wild`.
- Original `.config/opencode/tui.json` had `theme: "transparent"`, but also had legacy/unsupported keys: top-level `vim_system_clipboard_register`, `vim_enter_submit`, `prompt_max_height`, `prompt_scrollbar`, and keybinds `copy_mode`, `prompt_copy_selection`, `input_force_submit`.
- `.config/opencode/themes/transparent.json` uses `$schema: https://opencode.ai/theme.json`; important transparency values are `defs.none: "none"`, `panel: "none"`, `element: "none"`, `theme.background: "none"`, `backgroundPanel` pointing to panel/panelLight, and `backgroundElement` pointing to element/elementLight. The theme schema supports `"none"` for no color/terminal default, so the theme file itself is not the root cause.

Schema/doc validation:
- Fetched `https://opencode.ai/config.json`; current `opencode.json` schema has no TUI/theme fields, confirming TUI settings belong in `tui.json`.
- Fetched `https://opencode.ai/tui.json`; current TUI schema top-level relevant keys include only `$schema`, `theme`, `scroll_speed`, `scroll_acceleration`, `diff_style`, `mouse`, `keybinds`. It does not include top-level `vim_system_clipboard_register`, `vim_enter_submit`, `prompt_max_height`, or `prompt_scrollbar`.
- Current TUI keybind schema includes `input_submit`, `messages_copy`, `variant_list`, etc. It does not include legacy `input_force_submit`, `copy_mode`, or `prompt_copy_selection`.
- Fetched `https://opencode.ai/theme.json`; theme schema permits `"none"` and dark/light color objects, matching `themes/transparent.json`.

Runtime diagnosis:
- `command -v opencode` returned `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/bin/opencode`; `opencode --version` returned `1.14.48-ocv.3.50`.
- `ocv debug paths` showed OpenCode config path is `/Users/shahe.islam/.config/opencode`.
- `OPENCODE_PURE=1 ocv debug config | jq ...` confirmed resolved config loads model `openai/gpt-5.5`, small model `anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5`, plugins, permission, watcher, and agent settings without printing secrets.
- Grepping OpenCode logs under `/Users/shahe.islam/.local/share/opencode/log` found the root cause in recent logs, e.g. `/Users/shahe.islam/.local/share/opencode/log/2026-05-12T212336.log` and `2026-05-12T212311.log`: `WARN service=tui.config path=/Users/shahe.islam/.config/opencode/tui.json ... ConfigInvalidError ... message:"Unrecognized keybind: input_force_submit" ... invalid tui config`.
- Therefore OpenCode was rejecting the whole `tui.json`; because the invalid TUI config was ignored, `theme: "transparent"` never applied. There are also `opencode-pty` plugin incompatibility warnings (`Plugin requires opencode >=1.3.13 but running 1.14.48-ocv.3.50`) but those are separate from the transparent theme failure.

Edits already applied with `apply_patch`:
- Updated `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.config/opencode/tui.json`:
  - Removed unsupported top-level keys `vim_system_clipboard_register`, `vim_enter_submit`, `prompt_max_height`, `prompt_scrollbar`.
  - Renamed keybind `copy_mode` to `messages_copy` with value `<leader>v`.
  - Removed unsupported `prompt_copy_selection`.
  - Renamed `input_force_submit` to `input_submit` with value `alt+return`.
  - Kept `theme: "transparent"`, scrolling, `diff_style: "stacked"`, `mouse: false`, and the remaining valid keybinds.
- Updated `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/test-filter.sh` around the OpenCode tests:
  - Replaced old assertions for `vim_system_clipboard_register`, `vim_enter_submit`, `input_force_submit`, and `copy_mode`.
  - Added tests for TUI JSON validity, transparent theme, absence of legacy unsupported fields, `keybinds.input_submit == "alt+return"`, and `keybinds.messages_copy == "<leader>v"` while `variant_list == "<leader>V"` and no old `copy_mode`.
  - First test run after edits failed only on the new “OpenCode TUI avoids legacy unsupported fields” jq expression because of jq precedence. Patched that expression to `[has("vim_system_clipboard_register"), has("vim_enter_submit"), has("prompt_max_height"), has("prompt_scrollbar")] | any | not`.
  - Need rerun `scripts/test-filter.sh opencode` after this last jq patch.
- Updated `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/docs/opencode-nvim.md` line ~45 from claiming TUI owns removed Vim/prompt settings to: `.config/opencode/tui.json` owns supported TUI settings such as the transparent theme, scrolling, copy, and submit bindings.

Validation already done after editing:
- `curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/tui.json | jq --slurpfile cfg "$HOME/.config/opencode/tui.json" ...` checks for invalid top-level keys produced no output.
- Same schema-driven check for invalid keybind names produced no output.
- `jq empty "$HOME/.config/opencode/tui.json" "$HOME/.config/opencode/themes/transparent.json"` returned `json_valid=0` before edits; JSON remained valid after patches as part of test-filter partial run.
- Need rerun OpenCode filtered tests after final jq expression patch and optionally launch/check logs to confirm no fresh `invalid tui config` warning.

Symlink/stow findings:
- `~/.config/opencode -> ../dotfiles/.config/opencode` exists and is the key stow symlink for OpenCode. Because the directory is symlinked, files inside it (`tui.json`, `themes/transparent.json`, etc.) exist but are not individual symlinks. This is expected.
- Checks showed `~/.config` itself exists but is not a symlink, also expected.
- Checked older/singular theme paths: `~/.config/opencode/theme`, `~/.config/opencode/theme/tokyonight-transparent.json`, and `~/.config/opencode/opencode-theme.json` are missing. Actual source/live path is plural `themes/transparent.json`.
- Additional stow symlink status checks showed these missing targets: `~/bin/opencode`, `~/bin/open`, `~/bin/open-url`, `~/.config/codex-open-destination`, `~/.gemini/agents`, `~/.gemini/skills`, `~/.gitignore_local`.
- `stow --no --verbose .` from `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles` would link `.config/codex-open-destination`, `.gemini/agents`, `.gemini/skills`, `.gitignore_local`, `bin/open`, `bin/open-url`, `bin/opencode`; it also printed a suspicious `LINK: server exited unexpectedly => dotfiles/server exited unexpectedly`. It then aborted because of conflicts:
  - Existing real file `~/.gemini/settings.json` conflicts with repo source `.gemini/settings.json`; `cmp` result was 1 (different).
  - Existing real file `~/.ruff_cache/CACHEDIR.TAG` conflicts with repo source `.ruff_cache/CACHEDIR.TAG`; `cmp` result was 0 (identical cache file, should likely be ignored by stow).
  - Existing real file `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` conflicts with repo source `Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`; `cmp` result was 1 (different).
- Read repo source conflict files for context:
  - Dotfiles `.gemini/settings.json` contains Gemini hook wiring for `entire hooks gemini ...` across events (`AfterAgent`, `AfterModel`, `AfterTool`, `BeforeAgent`, `BeforeModel`, `BeforeTool`, `BeforeToolSelection`, `Notification`, `PreCompress`, `SessionEnd`, `SessionStart`) and `hooksConfig.enabled: true`.
  - Dotfiles `Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` contains MCP servers `playwright`, `steampipe`, `context7`, `drawio` using `bunx`, plus preferences `coworkScheduledTasksEnabled`, `ccdScheduledTasksEnabled`, `sidebarMode: chat`, `coworkWebSearchEnabled`, `floatingAtollActive`.
- Do not manually create symlinks. If resolving stow, use GNU Stow. Avoid `stow --adopt` or overwriting divergent real files without explicit user approval unless you can prove it is safe. A safe likely repo edit is to ignore `.ruff_cache` in `.stow-local-ignore` because it is a cache and currently causing a pointless conflict. The divergent `.gemini/settings.json` and Claude Desktop config require careful handling or user approval before replacement/adoption.

Pending next steps:
1. Rerun `scripts/test-filter.sh opencode` after the jq test patch.
2. Optionally run `ocv debug startup` or start a non-interactive-safe OpenCode diagnostic, then grep new logs to confirm no new `invalid tui config` warning. Avoid launching the full TUI if it may require raw TTY.
3. Handle stow symlink health: at minimum update `.stow-local-ignore` for `.ruff_cache` and rerun `stow --no --verbose .`; decide whether to ask user before adopting/replacing divergent `~/.gemini/settings.json` and `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`, or temporarily ignore those conflicts to install the missing unrelated symlinks.
4. Check git diff/status before final response.

parts.text:
Root cause: `~/.config/opencode/tui.json` was being rejected because it used legacy unsupported TUI keys, especially `input_force_submit`. Since OpenCode rejected the whole TUI config, `theme: "transparent"` was never applied.

Changed:
- `.config/opencode/tui.json`
  - Removed unsupported top-level keys.
  - Replaced `input_force_submit` with `input_submit`.
  - Replaced `copy_mode` with `messages_copy`.
  - Kept `theme: "transparent"`.
- `scripts/test-filter.sh`
  - Updated OpenCode tests to match the current TUI schema.
- `docs/opencode-nvim.md`
  - Updated docs to stop referencing removed TUI settings.
- `.stow-local-ignore`
  - Ignored `.ruff_cache`.
  - Ignored stray tracked root file `server exited unexpectedly` so stow does not try to link it into `~`.

Symlinks restored with GNU Stow:
- `~/.config/opencode -> ../dotfiles/.config/opencode`
- `~/bin/opencode -> ../dotfiles/bin/opencode`
- `~/bin/open -> ../dotfiles/bin/open`
- `~/bin/open-url -> ../dotfiles/bin/open-url`
- `~/.config/codex-open-destination -> ../dotfiles/.config/codex-open-destination`
- `~/.gemini/agents -> ../dotfiles/.gemini/agents`
- `~/.gemini/skills -> ../dotfiles/.gemini/skills`
- `~/.gitignore_local -> dotfiles/.gitignore_local`

Validated:
- `scripts/test-filter.sh opencode` passed: 117/117.
- `scripts/test-filter.sh stow` passed: 5/5.
- `OPENCODE_PURE=1 ocv debug startup --print-logs` no longer emits invalid TUI config errors.
- Latest OpenCode log has no `invalid tui config` / `input_force_submit` errors.

Remaining stow conflicts not overwritten:
- `~/.gemini/settings.json` differs from repo source.
- `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` differs from repo source.

Those need explicit approval before replacing/adopting because they are real divergent user config files.

Restart OpenCode for the transparent theme to take effect.
